title: fraxinus americana subsp. novae-angliae
intro: Fraxinus americana subsp. novae-angliae, also known as the New England ash, is a subspecies of the white ash tree (Fraxinus americana) native to the northeastern United States. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bark and leaves of the New England ash have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering numerous health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. New England ash can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile addition to herbal remedies.
